revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:長国寺 (台東区)]]{{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Chokoku-ji (Taito)04.jpg|religious_affiliation={{ill|Hokke-shū Honmon-ryū|qid=Q11555316|1=Hokke-shū Honmon-ryū}}|prefecture={{ill|Taitō-ku|qid=Q232641|1=Taitō}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.723|N|139.79197222222223|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}
{{Japanese temple infobox |name = Chōkoku-ji (長国寺) |image = [[File:Chokoku-ji (Taito).JPG|240px]] |location = Taito-ku, Tokyo, Japan |coordinates = {{Coord|35|43|23|N|139|47|30.1|E|type:landmark_region:JP-13|display=inline,title|qq=}} |mountain = Juzaisan (鷲在山) |sect = {{ill|Hokke Shū Honmon Ryū|ja|法華宗本門流|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokke Shū Honmon Ryū|1=Hokke-shū Honmon-ryū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11555316}} |status = |principal_image = {{ill|Ten Worlds Mandala|ja|十界曼荼羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten Worlds Mandala|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 法華曼荼羅|1=Lotus Mandala|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11555325}} |founded = [[Kanei]] 7 (1630) |founder = Nichiken (日乾) |patron = |map = Tokyo city }}{{Temple of Japan | Name = Chōkoku-ji | image = [[File:Chokoku-ji (Taito).JPG|240px]] | location = {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|qid=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}, {{ill|Taitō|en|Taitō|qid=Q232641|lt=Taitō Ward|1=Taitō}}, {{ill|Senzoku|ja|千束|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senzoku|1=Senzoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11405606}} 3-19-6 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|43|23|N|139|47|30.1|E|type:landmark_region:JP-13|display=inline,title|qq=}} | Mountain Name = Juzaisan (鷲在山) | Sect = {{ill|Hokkeshū Honmon Ryū|ja|法華宗本門流|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkeshū Honmon Ryū|1=Hokke-shū Honmon-ryū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11555316}} | Temple Rank = | Principal image = {{ill|Jikkai Mandala|ja|十界曼荼羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jikkai Mandala|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 法華曼荼羅|1=Lotus Mandala|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11555325}} | Year Founded = {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|qid=Q1192913|lt=Kan'ei era|1=Kan'ei}} 7 (1630) | Founder = Nichiken (日乾) | Patron = | Map = Tokyo city }} '''Chōkoku-ji''' (長国寺) is a {{ill|Buddhist temple|en|Buddhist temple|qid=Q5393308|lt=Buddhist temple|1=Buddhist temple}} located in {{ill|Senzoku|ja|千束|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senzoku|1=Senzoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11405606}}, {{ill|Taitō|en|Taitō|qid=Q232641|lt=Taitō Ward|1=Taitō}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|qid=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}. Affiliated with the {{ill|Hokkeshū Honmon Ryū|ja|法華宗本門流|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkeshū Honmon Ryū|1=Hokke-shū Honmon-ryū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11555316}}, the temple’s mountain name (''sangō'') is Juzaisan (鷲在山). It is a branch of the {{ill|Jusen-ji|lt=Jusen-ji|lt=Ōtori Temple|ja|鷲山寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Temple|qq=}} and is notable as the origin of the {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival in {{ill|Asakusa|en|Asakusa|qid=Q720644|lt=Asakusa|1=Asakusa}}. The temple is also referred to as ''Chōkokuji'' (長國寺). == History == The temple was founded in {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|qid=Q1192913|lt=Kanei|1=Kan'ei}} 7 (1630) by Nichiken (日乾), the 13th head priest of {{ill|Washūzan-ji|ja|鷲山寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washūzan-ji|1=Jusen-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11676248}}, who was said to be a descendant of {{ill|Ishida Mitsunari|en|Ishida Mitsunari|qid=Q334107|lt=Ishida Mitsunari|1=Ishida Mitsunari}}. Originally established in Torigoe Town, the temple was relocated to its current site near {{ill|Shin-Yoshiwara|ja|新吉原|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin-Yoshiwara|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉原 (東京都)|1=Yoshiwara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1859550}} in {{ill|Kanbun (era)|en|Kanbun (era)|qid=Q1146228|lt=Kanbun|1=Kanbun (era)}} 9 (1669). The principal deity is the {{ill|Myōken|en|Myōken|qid=Q10960623|lt=Great Bodhisattva of Washū Myōken|1=Myōken}}. Legend states that in {{ill|1265|en|1265|qid=Q5516|lt=1265|1=1265}}, Nichiren stayed at the Kobayakawa residence in Washisū, {{ill|Kazusa Province|en|Kazusa Province|qid=Q1055394|lt=Kazusa Province|1=Kazusa Province}} (now part of {{ill|Mobara|en|Mobara|qid=Q502486|lt=Mobara, Chiba|1=Mobara}}) and prayed for national peace. On the Tori day in November, it is said that Venus moved with mysterious power and appeared. In {{ill|1771|en|1771|qid=Q7686|lt=1771|1=1771}}, Nichigen enshrined this Washū Myōken from Washūzan-ji to Chōkoku-ji. At the time, the temple grounds included numerous buildings, and the shrine housing Washū Myōken was known by names such as ''Bangindō,'' ''Myōkendō,'' and ''Washū Daimyōjin.'' Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu bunri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離}} of the Meiji period, the temple grounds were divided, and Chōkoku-ji coexists with the nearby {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine (Taitō Ward)|ja|鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Taitō Ward)|qq=}}. Known as ''Washū Daimyōjin'' or ''Otorisama,'' this deity, depicted standing on the back of an eagle while holding a treasure sword, is revered as a patron deity for good fortune and prosperity. In particular, the ''Tori no Ichi'' festival, held in connection to the deity’s auspicious day, has been widely worshipped since the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}. The temple’s principal deity, often depicted standing atop a hawk and holding a treasure sword, is widely venerated as a protector of fortune and prosperity. As the spiritual centerpiece of Asakusa’s {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival, it has been revered since the Edo period.
== See Also == * {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine (Taitō Ward)|ja|鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Taitō Ward)|qq=}} * {{ill|Jusen-ji|lt=Jusen-ji|lt=Ōtori Temple|ja|鷲山寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Temple|qq=}} == Access == * 8-minute walk from {{ill|Iriya Station|en|Iriya Station|qid=Q845489|lt=Iriya Station|1=Iriya Station}} on the {{ill|Tokyo Metro Hibiya Line|en|Tokyo Metro Hibiya Line|qid=Q1196223|lt=Tokyo Metro Hibiya Line|1=Hibiya Line}} ({{OSM Route Map|start=35.72155,139.78545|goal=35.72301,139.79193|name=Route Guide|qq=}}). * 8-minute walk from {{ill|Minowa Station|en|Minowa Station|qid=Q1072979|lt=Minowa Station|1=Minowa Station}} on the {{ill|Tokyo Metro Hibiya Line|en|Tokyo Metro Hibiya Line|qid=Q1196223|lt=Tokyo Metro Hibiya Line|1=Hibiya Line}} ({{OSM Route Map|start=35.72878,139.79086|goal=35.72301,139.79193|name=Route Guide|qq=}}). == External links == * [http://otorisama.jp/ Official Website of Chōkoku-ji] {{Commonscat|qq=}} * == External Link == * [http://otorisama.jp/ Asakusa Tori-no-Tera: Juzaisan Chōkoku-ji Official Site]
{{translated page|ja|長国寺 (台東区)|version=90071029|comment=Imported full ja history}}
